notes: Long-report pagination fixed: footer always prints below the table, sections never strand their headers at a page bottom, and the Pk Hr label stays in its box.

changes: Fixed long-report (more than 2 hours) pagination on 3/4/5-leg: the contact footer no longer prints on top of the table — when a page is full, the footer now drops into the bottom margin so it sits on white space. The detection always existed but its result was silently discarded before saving; it also measured pages against an incomplete page-break list. Both fixed.

Added orphan protection for 3/4/5-leg data tables (previously only 6/7/8-leg had it): if a section's title and column headers would land at the bottom of a page with the data starting on the next page, the whole section now moves to the next page.
Fixed the Pk Hr / All / HV / HV% label graphic drifting below its box on long reports processed in parallel batches — the image is now re-pinned to its exact cell box right before saving.
Smoother progress bar: the per-job time estimate now scales with the study length, so 12-hour reports no longer rush the bar to 90% and then sit frozen for two minutes.
Multi-day studies now show ONE continuous progress bar across the whole run — previously the bar filled and restarted for every date's batch.
These pagination fixes apply to every layout and any study length (verified on 12-hour 48-interval reports and against all layout regression baselines).

Fixed a parallel-processing race that could corrupt one row of a longer-than-2-hour report's data tables (a row would show another table's cell layout — misaligned, italic, or ###### values). The format copy used the shared Windows clipboard, which the 4 parallel Excel workers could clobber mid-operation; it now uses a direct in-process copy, so neither the workers nor anything you copy-paste while a batch runs can corrupt a report.
New corruption scanner (tools/scan_table_corruption.py) that audits a whole Final Data folder for the above corruption signature in seconds.
Fixed packaged-app (EXE) crash on machines with the CountVault admin flag: the app tried to build the admin page from a module that doesn't ship in the EXE.

Fixed catastrophic packaged-app bug where starting a batch spawned an endless cascade of app windows (each background worker booted the full GUI) until the machine's display manager crashed. Worker processes now bootstrap correctly in the EXE.

Fixed 6/7/8-leg page margins randomly reverting to Excel defaults in batches (diagram bleeding off the page edge, overlapping tables, sliver pages). Margins are now forced to a known state every render, with a post-save verify-and-repair backstop.

Fixed 5-leg reports longer than 2 hours: detail-table rows now reproduce the template's own merged-cell layout (any leg count, any bin count) and stray borders between inserted intervals are cleaned up.
Vendor files marking an absent leg as 'No Leg' are now parsed correctly (a 7-leg site no longer renders as 8-leg).
Database re-renders now keep every leg's street label: 5/6/7/8-leg sites no longer collapse to a 4-leg diagram when rendered from Site History (schema v10 + backfill tool).
7-leg and 8-leg report generation is ~21% faster (cell writes batched into single operations), with smaller gains on 3/4/5-leg.

Download Reports dialog lists each collection window separately (a 6-9 AM + 11 AM-2 PM + 3-6 PM study shows three windows, never one '6 AM-6 PM' span); per-day select, format choice (Both/PDF/XLSX), and download-to-folder added.
Both PDF and XLSX can be downloaded together everywhere downloads exist (desktop site detail, portal site page with multi-select + per-day zips).
Site History trend chart gained 'All AM / Midday / PM periods' filters that compare like periods across collections (plotting peak-hour volume when windows differ, since totals aren't comparable).

QC map: top control bar no longer overlaps the zoom buttons or Views panel when the window narrows; rotate and move edits now persist in browser storage like resize already did; removed the non-functional Save QC Changes button.
New in-app update checker: the app now tells you when a new version is out, shows the full changelog, and downloads the update for you (this banner!).

Full 7-leg and 8-leg intersection support: page-1 diagrams plus complete data tables for all 7/8 directions and all 8 movements (hand-crafted wide templates; 7-leg reuses the 8-leg template with the unused block N/A'd).

Variable-length studies for 6/7/8-leg: data tables now grow or shrink to the actual interval count (anything from sub-hour up to a full 24 hours / 96 bins), matching the 4/5-leg behavior. Carries over automatically to database re-renders and custom-window reports.

Honest gapped-window display everywhere: a count collected 7-9 AM + 4-6 PM is shown and counted as two collection periods, never a fictitious continuous '7 AM - 6 PM'. Enforced by a build-time check so the bug class can't quietly return.
Per-window report rendering: gapped studies export one report per actual window (AM/MD/PM naming), in the app, the portal, and bulk zip downloads.
Graphical period-split editor: draggable timeline per file, split-in-half, apply one file's pattern to the rest, gap-aware clamping.

Speed and reliability: persistent pre-warmed worker pool (no more dead minute at batch start), outputs staged in %TEMP% to dodge antivirus races, automatic retry on transient Excel COM failures, headless-Edge render retry, and smooth monotonic progress reporting.

Maps: hybrid satellite with street and place labels, customizable heatmap panel (weight by counts / volume / peak, spread and intensity sliders), far less marker clustering, richer site popups with per-window stats and trend charts.
GIS export rebuilt on the real parser (raw vendor files now export correct volumes, multi-day averaging, AM/MD/PM peaks, auto-matched coordinates).
Kapturrit archive search, project-name overrides, on-call sub-project grouping, and the 'Open' button now opens the batch's actual output folder.

Site History database: every processed site is stored in a geographic database (sites cluster within 50 ft), with a full History tab — search, per-site detail, aliases, record cards, peak-volume trend charts, and an interactive all-sites map.

IDAX CountVault client portal: clients log in and see only their own counts — dashboard, sortable count tables, per-project rollups, site detail with downloads, map, and analytics. Access controlled by per-client grants with expiry.
Reports render straight from the database: any historical record can be re-rendered as XLSX/PDF on any machine, no original raw file needed (per-interval data stored at ingest).

Custom time-window reports: pick any clock window of a stored record and generate a fully recomputed report (stats, peak hour, PHF all recalculated). Single and bulk builders, cross-site bulk generation.
Per-record interval charts with per-direction / per-movement filtering and hover tooltips.
Cloud sync (Turso/libSQL) so the office desktop and laptop share one site-history database, with out-of-process sync that keeps the GUI responsive.
QC map state persistence (rotations, notes, verified flags) and the floating QC toolbar.
Batch client attribution tools, project-name parsing from folder structure, output de-duplication and attribution repair.
Initial release of the IDAX TMC Processor desktop app.

Processes raw vendor TMC files (standard and SDOT variants, auto-detected) into VBA-quality XLSX + PDF reports: intersection diagram page with movement counts, TEV, PHF, HV%/PHF table, and complete interval data tables with peak-hour highlighting.
All common layouts: 3-leg (4 orientations), 4-leg, 5-leg (4 diagonal variants), and 6-leg (6 variants), with per-layout reference templates and golden-image regression protection on page-1 geometry.
Peak hour engine matching the VBA macro exactly (rolling-hour windows, PHF, heavy-vehicle classes, bikes excluded from peaks, crosswalk peds separated).

Interactive QC map: per-site intersection diagrams (SVG) with volumes per movement, bike/ped insets, drag-to-rotate and move, multi-date and combined views, flow-balance checking between adjacent intersections.
Kapturrit integration: auto-detects the project from the folder path, matches sites to the Kapturrit map for coordinates, loads TMC Parent KML directly.
Automatic AM/MD/PM time-period splitting with gap detection, multi-day site matching, auto folder routing of outputs to the project's Final Data folder.
Parallel batch processing across worker processes with per-job status, plus GIS exports (GeoJSON, KML, CSV, Shapefile).
